title: Enthalpy (H)
short: The heat content of a system at constant pressure; a reaction's enthalpy change ΔH is the heat it absorbs (+) or releases (−).
full:
Enthalpy H is a state function — its value depends only on the current state, not on the path taken. Chemists almost never need H itself, only the change ΔH for a process at constant pressure, which equals the heat exchanged with the surroundings.

A negative ΔH marks an exothermic reaction (heat released, products lower in energy); a positive ΔH marks an endothermic one. Because enthalpy is a state function, ΔH for an overall reaction is the same no matter what route is taken — the basis of Hess's law.
